description: Models Section - Provider abstraction with dedicated Models page replacing Claude-specific settings

userStories.title: Create providers database table and migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new providers table to store provider configurations so that settings are namespaced per provider and extensible.
acceptanceCriteria: New `providers` table in backend/src/db/schema.ts with columns: id (INTEGER PRIMARY KEY), name (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL), runner_script (TEXT), is_configured (INTEGER DEFAULT 0), config (TEXT) for JSON
Add `provider` (TEXT, nullable) and `model_variant` (TEXT, nullable) columns to the projects table
Auto-migration on startup: reads claudeToken, claudeTokenType, claudeModel, autoMemoryEnabled from settings table, inserts 'claude' row into providers with migrated values, deletes old settings rows
During migration, all existing projects get provider='claude' and model_variant set to migrated claudeModel value (or provider default)
Migration is idempotent — re-running when data is already migrated does not duplicate or corrupt data

Seed Claude provider with runner_script='ralph-cc.sh' and config JSON containing modelVariants: ['claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514', 'claude-opus-4-5-20250514', 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001'], defaultVariant: 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514'
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in backend/)

userStories.title: Create provider abstraction interface and Claude provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a provider abstraction so that services can interact with any provider through a consistent interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/providers/types.ts with Provider interface: name, getEnvVars(config, modelVariant): Record<string, string>, getCliArgs(config, modelVariant): string[], getModelVariants(): string[], getAuthConfig(): { tokenType: string, tokenValue: string }, getFilesToSync(ralphPath: string): { source: string, dest: string }[]

Create backend/src/providers/claude.ts implementing Provider for Claude — returns correct env vars (ANTHROPIC_MODEL, CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY), CLI args (--model), model variants list, file sync paths
Create backend/src/providers/registry.ts with factory/registry: getProvider('claude') returns Claude instance
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in backend/)

userStories.title: Create /api/models backend routes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need new API routes under /api/models so the frontend can manage provider configurations through a clean, provider-namespaced API.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/routes/models.ts with Express Router
GET /api/models — returns list of all providers (id, name, runner_script, is_configured, parsed config JSON)
GET /api/models/:provider — returns single provider's full configuration
PUT /api/models/:provider/token — saves auth token (validates Claude format: must start with sk-ant-oat or sk-ant-api), updates is_configured, writes OAuth tokens to ~/.claude.json
DELETE /api/models/:provider/token — removes auth token, sets is_configured=0, for OAuth sets hasCompletedOnboarding: false in ~/.claude.json
PUT /api/models/:provider/model — saves selected model variant
DELETE /api/models/:provider/model — resets model variant to provider default
PUT /api/models/:provider/preferences — saves provider-specific behavioral preferences in config JSON
Register router in backend/src/index.ts at /api/models
Remove Claude-specific routes from backend/src/routes/settings.ts: claude-token (PUT/DELETE), claude-model (PUT/DELETE), auto-memory (PUT/DELETE)
Update GET /api/settings response to remove claudeConfigured, claudeModel, autoMemoryEnabled fields
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in backend/)

userStories.title: Refactor processManager to use provider abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the process manager to use the provider abstraction so that spawning runs works with any configured provider.
acceptanceCriteria: processManager.ts reads project's assigned provider and model_variant from projects table when starting a run
Uses provider registry to get correct provider instance
Calls provider.getEnvVars(config, modelVariant) to build environment variables instead of hardcoding Claude-specific env var names
Calls provider.getCliArgs(config, modelVariant) for CLI argument injection
Uses provider.runner_script from providers table to determine which script to spawn instead of hardcoding ralph-cc.sh
Git identity injection remains unchanged (not provider-specific)
Falls back gracefully if project has no provider assigned (logs error, does not crash)
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in backend/)

userStories.title: Refactor skillRunner to use provider abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the skill runner to use the provider abstraction so that skill execution uses the correct provider's settings.
acceptanceCriteria: skillRunner.ts accepts a project ID or provider name to determine which provider to use
Uses provider registry to get correct provider instance
Calls provider.getEnvVars(config, modelVariant) for environment variable injection
Calls provider.getCliArgs(config, modelVariant) for CLI argument building
Git identity injection remains unchanged
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in backend/)

userStories.title: Refactor fileCopier to use provider abstraction
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the file copier to use the provider abstraction so that each provider defines what files get synced.
acceptanceCriteria: fileCopier.ts accepts a provider name parameter when syncing files to a project
Calls provider.getFilesToSync(ralphPath) to get source/destination file paths instead of hardcoding SKILLS_TO_COPY and SCRIPTS_TO_COPY
Claude provider returns the same files currently synced: skill files (prd, prd-questions, ralph) and scripts (ralph-cc.sh, CLAUDE.md)
Executable permission (chmod 755) still applied to .sh files
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in backend/)

userStories.title: Update frontend API layer for models
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the frontend API layer updated to call the new /api/models endpoints instead of the old /api/settings/claude-* endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: New API functions in frontend/src/lib/api.ts: getModels(), getProvider(name), saveProviderToken(provider, token), deleteProviderToken(provider), saveProviderModel(provider, model), deleteProviderModel(provider), saveProviderPreferences(provider, prefs)
New API functions for per-project provider: saveProjectProvider(projectId, provider), saveProjectModelVariant(projectId, variant)
New TypeScript interfaces: ProviderResponse (id, name, runner_script, is_configured, config), ModelsResponse (array of providers)
Remove old Claude-specific API functions: saveClaudeToken, deleteClaudeToken, saveClaudeModel, deleteClaudeModel, saveAutoMemory, deleteAutoMemory
Update ProjectSummary interface to include provider: string | null and model_variant: string | null
Update SettingsResponse interface to remove claudeConfigured, claudeModel, autoMemoryEnabled fields
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in frontend/)

userStories.title: Create Models page with tabbed layout and Claude tab
userStories.description: As a user, I want a dedicated Models page with a tabbed interface so I can configure my AI provider settings in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: New page component at frontend/src/pages/Models.tsx
Page title 'Models' with consistent styling matching Settings page header
Tab bar with single 'Claude' tab (establishes pattern for future providers)

Claude tab has three sections: (1) Authentication — token input with show/hide toggle and save/delete buttons, 'Configured' badge when token is set; (2) Model Selection — dropdown with presets (Sonnet, Opus, Haiku) and custom model ID option; (3) Preferences — auto-memory toggle checkbox
All functionality matches current Settings page behavior (same validation, same success/error messages)
Uses React Query to fetch from GET /api/models/claude and mutations for PUT/DELETE endpoints
Route added in App.tsx: /models renders <Models />
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in frontend/)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Remove Claude settings from Settings page
userStories.description: As a user, I want the Settings page to only show non-provider settings so there's no confusion about where to configure AI models.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings page no longer shows: Claude Model selection, Claude Authentication, Auto-Memory toggle
Settings page retains: Ralph Installation Path, Git Configuration (Docker-only)
No broken references or dead code left behind in Settings.tsx
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in frontend/)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add per-project provider and model variant selection to Kanban page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to assign a specific AI provider and model variant to each project so different projects can use different models.
acceptanceCriteria: Project Kanban page header shows a provider dropdown and a model variant dropdown
Provider dropdown lists all configured providers from GET /api/models
Model variant dropdown options change based on selected provider (selecting Claude shows Sonnet/Opus/Haiku)
Selecting a provider saves immediately via PUT /api/projects/:id/provider
Selecting a model variant saves immediately via PUT /api/projects/:id/model-variant
When provider is changed, model variant resets to that provider's default
Backend routes added: PUT /api/projects/:id/provider and PUT /api/projects/:id/model-variant
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in frontend/ and backend/)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Models to sidebar navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see 'Models' in the sidebar so I can navigate to the new section easily.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Models' nav link appears between 'Projects' and 'Settings' in the sidebar
Uses a brain/AI SVG icon consistent with existing icon style (stroke-based, 20x20)
Active state uses bg-ralph-600/20 text-ralph-300 styling (same as other nav items)
Works on both desktop sidebar and mobile drawer
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in frontend/)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Show no-provider banner and block runs for unassigned projects
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a clear message when a project has no provider assigned, and runs should be blocked until one is selected.
acceptanceCriteria: When project has no provider assigned (provider is null), prominent banner at top of Kanban page: 'No model provider assigned — select one to start runs' with inline provider dropdown
Start Run button is disabled when no provider is assigned
After selecting provider from banner dropdown, banner disappears and run button becomes enabled
Backend POST /api/runner/start returns 400 if project has no provider assigned
Typecheck passes (`npm run build` in frontend/ and backend/)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
